items.headline: Claude now shows you a monthly recap of your own AI habits
items.summary: Anthropic launched Reflect on July 9, a beta dashboard (Settings > Reflect) that breaks down what you've been using Claude for, in categories like research, writing, and technical questions, plus your busiest days and hours. It includes a "quiet hours" scheduler and periodic break reminders, and requires Claude's memory feature to be turned on.
items.why_it_matters: If you've ever wondered whether Claude is actually saving you time in your business or just becoming a new thing to check, this gives you real numbers instead of a guess, though it's worth remembering the dashboard also nudges you to use Claude more, so treat its suggestions as marketing, not advice.

items.headline: Anthropic wiped everyone's Claude Code usage limits again, the fourth reset in two months
items.summary: On July 9, Anthropic's Claude Code team posted that it had reset the 5-hour and weekly usage limits for every Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise user, giving everyone a clean slate. It's the latest in a string of resets following a rough stretch of outages and bugs that had been burning through people's usage faster than expected.
items.why_it_matters: If you use Claude Code to build or tweak parts of your website or workflows and have been rationing your usage, your allowance is freshly topped up right now, so this is a good week to tackle something you've been putting off.

items.headline: OpenAI's biggest ChatGPT upgrade of the year is out, with a new agent that can run projects for you
items.summary: OpenAI made its new GPT-5.6 model family (three tiers: Sol, Terra, and Luna) generally available on July 9 across ChatGPT, the API, and Codex, alongside a new tool called ChatGPT Work, an agent mode that can spend hours building spreadsheets, slide decks, and simple web apps from your connected files and accounts.
items.why_it_matters: If you already use ChatGPT for your business, you should notice snappier, more capable answers this week at no extra cost, and ChatGPT Work is worth a cautious test on something low-stakes, like a first-draft project spreadsheet, just review anything it produces before it goes anywhere near a client.

categories.title: Tools & Tips for Creators and Small Biz

items.headline: CapCut's AI image tool just got a major upgrade, including text that doesn't look broken
items.summary: ByteDance, CapCut's parent company, launched Seedream 5.0 Pro on July 9 inside CapCut's Dreamina image tool. The upgrade can render clean, readable text in over a dozen languages (a problem that's tripped up nearly every AI image generator so far), lets you edit one specific part of an image instead of regenerating the whole thing, and outputs at higher resolution for use as thumbnails or video keyframes.
items.why_it_matters: If you've tried AI-generated thumbnails or graphics before and given up because the text came out as garbled nonsense, this is worth a second try, since spelling your channel name or a call-to-action correctly was exactly the weak spot this update targets.

items.headline: Meta can now use your public Instagram photos to generate AI images of you, unless you turn it off
items.summary: Meta's new Muse Image tool, live since July 7, lets anyone tag a public Instagram account in a prompt and generate an AI image using that person's likeness. The setting is turned on by default for adult public accounts. Opting out (Profile > Menu > Sharing and reuse, then turn off the Posts and Reels toggles) stops future use, but doesn't remove any AI images already made before you switched it off.
items.why_it_matters: If your Instagram is public for business reasons, check this setting today, since Meta won't notify you when someone uses your photos this way, and anything generated before you opt out stays in circulation.

opportunity.action_note: Open Instagram, go to Profile > Menu > Sharing and reuse, and turn off the Posts and Reels toggles under AI features today, before someone else uses your public photos to generate an AI image of you.
opportunity.angle: Meta is letting people make AI images of you from your public Instagram photos, here's the one setting to check right now

items.headline: X built a real video editor into its iOS app, with captions and a green screen
items.summary: X rolled out a new Video Editor and Recorder inside its iOS app on July 7, adding multilingual captions you can restyle on screen, a green screen tool for swapping in custom backgrounds, and segmented recording. The goal is getting creators to film and post original video without leaving the app.
items.why_it_matters: If you post on X alongside YouTube and Instagram, this is a free way to knock out a quick captioned clip straight from your phone without opening a separate editing app, though it's iOS-only for now.
